name: Strike price notation
description: Strike price notation describes the strike price in terms of its units.
optionalityRules: Required if Strike price, Lower or only barrier price and Upper barrier price, respectively, is reported.
Values are not reported for the not-reported Action types.
valueRules: Reported as ‘1’ where the strike price or barrier price is reported as a monetary amount; or ‘3’ where the strike price or barrier price is reported as a decimal.

In alignment with the requirements in other major jurisdictions, notation should be reported as: ‘1’ (i.e. a monetary amount) for equity and debt security options, commodity options, foreign exchange options and similar products; and ‘3’ (i.e. a decimal) for interest rate options, interest rate and credit swaptions quoted in spread terms, and similar products.
Currently either notation of ‘1’ or ‘3’ may be reported for FX options, but reporting entities are encouraged to change to reporting with notation ‘1’ as soon as practically possible.
If reported, the formats of the values conform to the 2024 Rules specifications.
